summary: NextAuth Credentials authentication testing completed for LMS CozyTech Next.js 16 app. All 11 auth features tested and working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: NextAuth v5 (beta.31) properly configured with Credentials provider
JWT callbacks correctly store name, email, role, id in session
Middleware uses Edge-compatible auth.config.ts for route protection
Role-based access control working: admin can access /admin and /dashboard, student can only access /dashboard
Login page has proper data-testid attributes for testing
Demo quick-fill buttons work correctly for both admin and student accounts
seed_data_creation: Seed endpoint at POST /api/seed creates admin and student users with bcrypt hashed passwords
context_for_next_testing_agent: All NextAuth authentication features tested and working. Login page at /login with email/password fields and demo quick-fill buttons. Admin redirects to /admin, student redirects to /dashboard. Middleware protects /dashboard (any logged-in user) and /admin (admin role only). Session contains name, email, role, id fields.
tests_passed: Login page renders at /login with email and password fields
Login as admin@mail.com/123456 succeeds and redirects to /admin
Login as user@mail.com/123456 succeeds and redirects to /dashboard
Invalid credentials show error message on login page
Session contains name, email, role fields after login
Unauthenticated user accessing /dashboard gets redirected to /login
Unauthenticated user accessing /admin gets redirected to /login
Demo account quick-fill buttons on login page work
api_tests_passed: GET /api/auth/session returns null when not authenticated
POST /api/auth/callback/credentials with admin credentials creates session with role=admin
POST /api/auth/callback/credentials with student credentials creates session with role=student
Invalid credentials redirect with error parameter
Empty credentials redirect with error parameter
